query: What is Elon Musk's current net worth according to Bloomberg Billionaires Index in early July 2026? Has it recovered above $1 trillion after the late June 2026 tech selloff? Include recent Tesla and SpaceX share price and valuation trajectory.
answer:
I've completed the research. Here's a summary of what I found:

## Key Findings

**Bloomberg Net Worth (early July 2026): ~$932 billion — BELOW $1 trillion.** Musk has NOT durably recovered above the trillion-dollar mark since the late-June selloff.

**Timeline:**
- **June 12, 2026:** Became the world's first trillionaire (~$1.1T) upon SpaceX's record IPO
- **~June 16:** Peaked at an intraday ~$1.45 trillion
- **June 22–24:** Late-June tech selloff cut him below $1T; Bloomberg had him at ~$957B on June 24 (a June 22 SpaceX plunge alone erased ~$240B, compounded by restrictions on ~$116B of his Tesla shares)
- **Early July:** ~$932B (Bloomberg) / ~$916B (Forbes)

**Source caveat:** Bloomberg's official close-of-day figure is the conservative anchor (~$932B). Some real-time third-party trackers briefly showed ~$1.07T on July 9, but both Bloomberg and Forbes place him just under $1T.

**Tesla (TSLA):** $406.55 (July 9 close), market cap ~$1.53T, ~18.5% below its 52-wk high of $498.83. Bottomed at $375.12 (June 25), rebounded to $425.30 (July 1), then choppy.

**SpaceX (SPCX):** $152.16 (July 9 close). IPO'd at $135 June 12, peaked at $211.39 (June 16), fell ~28%, and briefly neared its IPO price. Joined the Nasdaq-100 on July 7 but slid on "buy the rumor, sell the news" plus a data-center lawsuit threatening a $45B Anthropic contract.

Musk remains the world's richest person by a wide margin (Larry Page second at ~$296B). The full synthesis and structured data have been recorded.

key_facts: Bloomberg Billionaires Index puts Musk's net worth at ~$932 billion as of July 2026 — below the $1 trillion mark (Forbes ~$916B).
Musk became the world's first trillionaire on June 12, 2026, when SpaceX completed the largest IPO in history.
His net worth peaked at an intraday ~$1.45 trillion around June 16, 2026, the week after the IPO.
He lost trillionaire status during the late-June 2026 tech selloff; Bloomberg had him at ~$957B on June 24, 2026.
A 16% single-day SpaceX plunge on June 22, 2026 erased roughly $240 billion from Musk's fortune.
New restrictions on ~$116 billion of Musk's Tesla shares also weighed on Bloomberg's calculation.
As of early July 2026, his net worth has NOT durably recovered above $1 trillion per Bloomberg, though real-time trackers briefly showed ~$1.07T (July 9).
Tesla (TSLA) closed at $406.55 on July 9, 2026, market cap ~$1.53T, ~18.5% below its 52-week high of $498.83.
TSLA hit a selloff low of $375.12 on June 25, 2026, and rebounded to $425.30 on July 1.
SpaceX (SPCX) IPO'd June 12, 2026 at $135/share; opened ~$150, closed ~$161 (~$2.1T valuation).
SpaceX all-time closing high was $211.39 on June 16, 2026; it closed at $152.16 on July 9, 2026 (~28% below peak).
SpaceX joined the Nasdaq-100 on July 7, 2026, but fell toward its IPO price amid 'buy the rumor, sell the news' and a data-center lawsuit threatening a $45B Anthropic contract.
Musk owns ~42% of SpaceX and ~20% of Tesla; he remains the world's richest person (Larry Page second ~$296B).
